title: [Bug]: Cache miss on `ARG`
body:
### Issue Description

I have gitlab CI traces here of a cache miss on an `ARG` line that hasn't changed. You can see in the logs that all lines before the `ARG` are considered cached, but that is the one that busts it. These runs happened less than ten minutes apart.

[0.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/10373684/0.log)
[1.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/10373686/1.log)


### Steps to reproduce the issue

I've attached the Dockerfile in question. 
[Dockerfile.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/10373711/Dockerfile.txt)

As you can see in the traces, the build command is:
```
podman build --build-arg RUST_TOOLCHAIN -t $TEST_IMAGE -f Dockerfile.test --cache-from $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/cache --cache-to $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/cache .
```

under the environment variable `RUST_TOOLCHAIN=nightly-bullseye-slim-2023-01-01`.

### Describe the results you received

Busted cache.

### Describe the results you expected

Unbusted cache.

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Gitlab CI shared runners running `quay.io/podman/stable`

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Hi @SohumB , I think this must be fixed here https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4326 but looks like its not released here.

I see. I tried using `quay.io/buildah/stable` i.e. buildah v1.28, which was released after the PR linked on that issue was closed, but it still seemed to have the same issue:
```
$ buildah build --build-arg RUST_TOOLCHAIN -t $TEST_PREPARE_IMAGE -f .meta/Dockerfile.test --cache-from $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/cache --cache-to $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/cache --target prepare .
[...]
[1/2] STEP 5/18: ENV SCCACHE_DIR=/usr/local/sccache
--> Cache pulled from remote registry.gitlab.com/climaterisk/facades/cache:988897c367dee93e6f33125237fe48feb3a901b66defadd12681c6caf3c6394a
--> Using cache 32499281d287675be0b33a3d36d5c891ecf58fdcb94ea87a72d63d56e668c6b5
--> 32499281d28
[1/2] STEP 6/18: ARG TARGET=x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
--> Pushing cache registry.gitlab.com/climaterisk/facades/cache:e9ad1722253f17e3d346a4990a2e67c90964533d1fea7a8ae915a2b4d0621e41
--> ab0839d26b4
```

(the differences in steps are because my Dockerfile has also changed some since, but this is the second run with the same file.)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Hi!

I started using podman (thanks for the hard work 🙇 ) recently and just stumbled upon this issue. 
I can't find any related issue/PR on the buildah repo besides https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4326 which has been merged end of October 2022.

Is it tracked somewhere else?
Hi @Sankluj and @SohumB , Could you please share a small reproducer for this issue, is it different from the test added in https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4326 ?

I don't _imagine_ the linked reproducer would be a different issue from this one, but unfortunately I can't say for sure — I don't know if there are more complicated conditions under which `ARG` would bust the cache. That said, I've now noticed that that commit wasn't actually in v1.28 — it's in v1.29.1 — so I'll test that, and if I still get a busted cache, I'll try to find a smaller repro.
I can confirm that my case is solved on 1.29.1!
@Sankluj Could you also please try the latest version as @SohumB, if it works for you I'll close this issue.

I think similar issue was reported was reported here https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4621 and its fixed on`main` https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4621. I am closing this issue but please feel free to re-open if it this is not resolved.
